label: Loop
description: Iterate over a collection by re-executing the downstream subgraph once per item, with the iteration variable bound in the per-iteration input.

settings.name: iterateOver
settings.label: Collection
settings.description: Path to the array to iterate over, e.g. 'input.items' or 'steps.fetch.output'. When unset, the loop iterates over the upstream input directly.

settings.name: itemVariable
settings.label: Item Variable
settings.description: Name of the per-iteration variable bound on each loop body run (defaults to 'item').

ai.shortDescription: Iterate over an array, running the downstream subgraph once per item.
ai.whenToUse: When you need to apply the same step (or chain of steps) to each element of a list — e.g. enrich every record returned by an upstream query.
ai.examplePrompt: for each item in input.records, run the LLM summarisation step
ai.promptHints: Set iterateOver to the dotted path of the array (often 'input.items' or 'steps.X.output'). Inside the loop body, reference the current element with `input` (primitives) or input.<itemVariable> (objects).

outputAssertions.name: loop_result_shape
outputAssertions.errorMessage: Loop node returned a malformed result — expected { iterations: unknown[], itemCount: number }. Empty arrays are a valid outcome (itemCount: 0, iterations: []).
